title: TRANSITIONAL LIVING - 211info
text: BOYS & GIRLS AID BEAVERTON TRANSITIONAL LIVING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary housing and support for homeless youth and young adults. The program is designed for young people to work with staff in learning life skills for independent living. Eligibility: Ages 18-24, homeless or a history of housing instability, fleeing domestic violence, low income as defined by federal guidelines Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-5pm, Tuesday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 542-2350 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (503) 542-2350 Other (971) 427-4223 Mitch Lemley [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None AREA SERVED: Washington County Services: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Youth Youth Shelters * Homeless Youth If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
